Q3 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The forward current in a silicon diode is 15 mA at 27°C. If reverse saturation current is 0.24 nA, the forward bias voltage V is ________ V. (upto three decimal places)
(Assume, η = 2)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.928
Solution

0.928 (0.800 - 1.000)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

An n-channel MOSFET requires overdrive voltage of 1 V to get the drain current of 1 mA. If the drain current required is 2 mA, then new overdrive voltage will be
(Assume MOSFET to be in saturation region)

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A MOS capacitor has an oxide layer thickness of 400 and flat band voltage of –1.5 V. If the effective positive charge at the semiconductor-oxide layer interface is Qs = 4.5 × 10–8 C/cm2, the metal semiconductor work function potential difference is __________ V.
(Assume εox = 4 ε0 and ε0 = 8.85 × 10–12 F/m)

NAT
Correct answer: -0.992
Solution

-0.992 (-1.25 - -0.75)

From the above graph, the relation between T1 and T2 holds good for (where T : temperature (in degrees)).
Correct answer: B

then the average time taken by holes to travel a distance of 0.5 μm in this Ge sample is

Correct answer: C

Q8 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In a silicon semiconductor at T = 300 K, if the fermi energy is 0.22 eV above the valence band energy, then the approximate electron concentration (n0) in semiconductor is equal to _________ × 105 cm–3. (upto three decimal places)
(Assume, intrinsic carrier concentration of silicon, ni = 1.5 × 1010 cm–3, density of states in valence band, NV = 1.04 × 1019 cm–3, kT = 0.0259 eV)

NAT
Correct answer: 1.057
Solution

1.057 (1.000 - 1.150)

 
 
Q10 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A silicon diode at an ambient temperature of 25°C has cut-in voltage of 0.65 V. If the junction temperature is increased to 35°C, then the percentage change in the cut-in voltage will be

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q11 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The temperature coefficient of resistivity in intrinsic semiconductor is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q12 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A silicon sample doped with n-type impurities at 1018 cm–3 has a resistance of 10 Ω. The silicon sample has an area of 10–6 cm2 and the length of 10 μm. Given that the electron mobility ‘μn’ is 800 cm2/V-s, the doping efficiency of the sample is _________% (upto one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 78.1
Solution

78.1 (76.0 - 79.0)

 
 
Q13 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A step graded silicon PN junction diode at room temperature under equilibrium conditions has a p-side doping concentration of NA = 2 × 1015/cm3 and built in potential across the junction is 0.6 V. Then doping concentration on n-side (ND) is ______ × 1015/cm3.
(Assume VT = 26 mV, intrinsic carrier concentration of Si is ni = 1.5 × 1010 cm–3 at room temperature).

NAT
Correct answer: 1.184
Solution

1.184 (1.10 - 1.30)

 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The depletion width of an abrupt p+n junction is 0.457 μm and penetration of the depletion region into p-side is 0.00457 μm. Then the ratio of acceptor concentration on p+- side to donor concentration on n-side is __________.
(Assume under thermal equilibrium condition).

NAT
Correct answer: 99
Solution

99 (98 - 100)

 
 
Q15 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Excess electrons are generated in a semiconductor to a concentration of 1016 cm–3. The excess carrier lifetime in the semiconductor is 5 × 10–6 sec. The source generating the excess carriers is switched off at t = 0. The recombination rate of excess electrons at t = 5 μsec is
________ × 1021 cm–3 s–1. (upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.74
Solution

0.74 (0.60 - 0.80)

 
Q16 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

An n-type silicon with doping concentration ND = 1016 cm–3 is given. If this sample is uniformly illuminated, the steady state minority concentration in the sample will be 4 × 104 cm–3. Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?
(Assume ni = 1.5 × 1010 cm–3 and τp = 10–6 sec)

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c)

Correct answer: D
Solution

Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

field that appears across the junction is
(Assume VT = 26 mV, ni = 1.5 × 1010 cm–3)

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

(Assume planck’s constant h = 6.6 × 10–34 J-s, c = 3 × 108 m/sec and quantum efficiency is 100%)

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

At 300 K, a 0.01 cm2 ideal Si p-n junction solar cell has a short-circuit current of 2A and an opencircuit voltage of 0.5 V. The maximum output power density of the solar cell is 1020 W/cm2. Then fill factor is equal to _________ (upto one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 10.2
Solution

10.2 (9.0 - 11.0)

NAT
Correct answer: 10.35

10.35 (10.10 - 10.50)

Correct answer: 3.85

3.85 (3.70 - 4.00)

(upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 1116.07

1116.07 (1114.00 - 1118.00)
